title: Sequence Specialist Blended With GBDT
summary: Recent F1 pit-window studies found deep sequential models competitive because cumulative degradation is inherently temporal.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-window studies found deep sequential models competitive because cumulative degradation is inherently temporal. The current best solutions are feature-engineered tabular transforms; a compact sequence model could capture nonlinear stint evolution missed by hand-built rolling stats.
implementation_hint: Build short per-driver-per-race sequences ending at the current lap and train a small GRU/TCN/Transformer on ordered numeric inputs plus embeddings for race/compound/driver. Use grouped OOF predictions as an additional feature or average with the best CatBoost/LightGBM model rather than replacing the baseline.
expected_effect: May capture nonlinear pre-stop patterns such as sudden pace fade, stabilization after traffic, or compound-specific warmup effects that simple roll3 features smooth away.
risk: Sequence nets can overfit to driver/race identity and usually need stricter grouped validation; if trained end-to-end on full rows without careful masking, leakage is easy.
